trialDescriptions.title: Klinische Studie zur Detektion einer kardialen Dekompensation mittels monti®-Plattform unter Verwendung eines AI-Modells bei Patienten mit Herzinsuffizienz
trialDescriptions.summary:
Das Krankheitsbild der Herzinsuffizienz und eine damit verbundene kardiale Dekompensation ("Überwässerung")  ist der häufigste Grund für eine stationäre Krankenhausbehandlung. Zudem steigt mit jeder Dekompensation, die im Krankenhaus behandelt werden muss, das Risiko an einer Herzinsuffizienz zu versterben.
Daher ist es wichtig, Überwässerungen frühzeitig zu erkennen, um eine Krankenhausbehandlung zu vermeiden. Kleine, tragbare Geräte, sogenannte „Wearables“, ermöglichen ein einfaches, kontinuierliches Monitoring von Vitalparametern und körperlicher Aktivität von Zuhause aus. Ziel unserer Studie ist es, mittels dieser Daten und maschinellem Lernen herauszufinden, inwieweit eine drohende Überwässerung vorhergesagt werden kann.
Die über das verwendete Wearable ("Corsano CardioWatch 287-2") gesammelten Daten werden an eine Datenmanagement-Platform ("monti") mittels Smartphone-Applikation ("monti-App") übermittelt. Auf Grundlage dieser Daten wird dann ein AI-basierter Gefährdungsindex berechnet.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary:
Heart failure (HF) is the most common cause of hospitalization and in-hospital death in Germany. Recurrent hospitalizations due to HF increase the risk of adverse events and are associated with high mortality rates. Heart failure-induced hospitalizations are driven by up to 70% by acutely decompensated HF (ADHF). There is a need for innovative and individualized concepts for preemptive detection of congestion in patients with HF that reduces the burden on the healthcare system. Implementation of digital tools for assessing early ADHF represents an option for the management of patients with HF and potentially facilitates early treatment interventions. In a previous study, we demonstrated the feasibility of detecting clinical conditions leveraging wearable data obtained in oncology patients undergoing chemotherapy under in- and outpatient conditions, by creating a risk index using deep learning analysis (“self-supervised contrastive learning”). For the CONAN study, we hypothesized that a similar self-supervised contrastive learning-derived risk index enables reliable detection of episodes with ADHF.
In- and outpatients with ADHF will consecutively be screened for in- and exclusion criteria at the cardiology department at University Hospital RWTH Aachen. All patients will be treated according to the standard of care, and the study visits will be scheduled accordingly. The monitoring period starts from the inclusion of the patients into the study followed by continuous monitoring by the wearable for 90 days. Routine outpatient visits at the heart failure outpatient clinic will be scheduled approximately 14 and 90 days post-decompensation. Additional show-ups at the outpatient clinic of the Department of Cardiology at the University Hospital RWTH Aachen will be considered study visits.
The baseline visit of a given patient will be conducted within the initial 24 hours after diagnose of ADHF. During the baseline visit, patients will receive study equipment, including a smartphone and the CardioWatch 287-2 wrist-wearable (Corsano Health B.B., Isaac da Costalaan 20, 1401 BH Bussum, The Netherlands) for the monitoring period. Additionally, the following data will be obtained: medical history including HF entity, comorbidities, symptoms, signs, vital signs and laboratory values Bioelectrical impedance analysis and EVEREST Score, an established clinical tool for assessing congestion will be performed.[13] Three paper-based validated questionnaires will be used to assess digital health literacy (HLS19-DIGI) overall, self-care behavior in heart failure using the 9-item European Heart Failure Self-Care Behaviour Scale (EHFScB-9), and the Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire (KCCQ-12), which assesses the impact of heart failure on different facets of life.
During follow-up visits, symptoms, signs, vital signs vital signs, as well as concise safety and deficiencies assessment will be assessed. Moreover, details regarding HF-related, all unplanned, non-trauma-related hospitalizations and emergency department visits will also be recorded. During Visit 1, the first 10 patients will complete the System Usability Scale (SUS) to identify any potential deficits in the monitoring platform that can be iteratively optimized. 
The end-of-study visit will be scheduled on Day 90 post-decompensation, with a window of plus or minus 14 days. EHFScB-9 and KCCQ-12 questionnaires are completed by the patients again. Additionally, the Mobile App Rating Scale (MARS-G) and SUS questionnaires will be handed out to the patients. 
Data will only be analyzed retrospectively, therefore there will be no clinical action taken by the recorded data.

trialDescriptions.title: CONgestion Detection using the monti®-PlAtform with an Integrated Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning-Derived Risk Index in Patients with CoNgestive Heart Failure

observationalGroups.comment: Patienten mit akut dekompensierter Herzinsuffizienz unter kontinuierlichem Monitoring mittels monti®-Plattform für 90 Tage
Patients with Congestive Heart Failure under continuous monitoring via monti®-Platform for 90 days

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: - Berechnung der AUROC ("Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristics curve") zur Bewertung der Detektionsfähigkeit von Episoden mit akuter dekompensierter Herzinsuffizienz im Sinne eines Gefährdungsindex unter Verwendung der monti®-Plattform  und eines AI-Modells
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome: - Bewertung der Datenintegrität der monti®-Plattform und der Qualität der  mobilen monti-App (durchschnittlicher Prozentsatz der übermittelten Daten pro Tag, Zeitunterschied zwischen dem protokollierten Messzeitpunkt und der Datenbank, Bewertung auf der Mobilen App-Bewertungsskala (MARS-G))

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: - Calculating AUROC ("Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristics curve") to assess the detection capabilities of episodes with ADHF by the self-supervised contrastive learning-derived risk index based on wearable recorded time series
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome: - Assessing data integrity of the monti®-platform and quality of the mobile monti-app (mean % of wearable data per day, time difference between logged time point of measurement and database, score in Mobile App Rating Scale (MARS-G))

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria:
- Aktive maligne Erkrankung
- Schwangerschaft
- Aktive schwere, systemische Infektion, welche eine intravenöse antibiotische Therapie erforderlich macht
- Terminale Niereninsuffizienz mit Dialysepflichtigkeit
- Mentale Einschränkungen (z.B. durch Demenz, Medikamentennebenwirkungen), welche eine kontinuierliche Nutzung des Wearables bzw. der Monitoring-Plattform behindert
- Einschränkungen, die das Tragen des Wearables unmöglich machen (z.B. Shunt, Blutungen, Amputationen)
- Mentale Einschränkungen oder Sprachbarrieren, die das Studienverständnis und die Kooperation beeinträchtigen, sodass ein erfolgreicher Studienabschluss nach Einschätzung des Prüfarztes nicht wahrscheinlich erscheint
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria:
- Akut dekompensierte Herzinsuffizienz mit Diagnose innerhalb der letzten 24 Stunden
- Alter >= 18 Jahre und Verständnis des Studienaufbaus und der Ziele
- Unterschriebene Einverständniserklärung und Einwilligung in die Datenschutzerklärung
- Bereitschaft zum Tragen des Wearables während des Studienzeitraums und Teilnahme an den Verlaufsvisiten

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria:
- Active malignant disease
- Pregnancy
- Active severe systemic infection, defined as intravenous administration of antibiotics
- End stage renal disease with dialysis
- Medical or mental conditions, such as dementia, that hinder the continuous use of the monitoring platform and associated equipment during the monitoring period
- Any condition that makes wearing the wearable device impossible (e.g., presence of a shunt, active bleeding, or absence of limbs)
- Mental incapacity or language barriers which preclude adequate understanding or cooperation, known or suspected not to comply with study directives or not to be reliable or trustworthy, or subject who in the opinion of the investigator should not participate in the study
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria:
- Acute-decompensated heart failure confirmed within the last 24 hours
- Age >= 18 years and able to understand design and objectives of the trial
- Signed written informed consent and data safety agreement before any study-related activities
- Willingness to wear the wearable during the treatment period and return it on a follow-up visit

contact.affiliation: Ethik-Kommission an der Medizinischen Fakultät der RWTH Aachen
